title: acetosella glandulosa
intro: Acetosella glandulosa, also known as cranberry or lingonberry, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to northern Europe and Asia, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and red or purple berries. The medicinal properties of Acetosella glandulosa lie in its high concentration of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, particularly vitamin C and manganese, which exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to prevent and treat urinary tract infections, reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. Acetosella glandulosa can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or supplement, and is often used in traditional medicine to support overall health and well-being.
